content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

content.content:
1→# bootstrap
     2→
     3→One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs. Goes from archinstall to private repo access with a single command and one vault password.
     4→
     5→## Usage
     6→
     7→```bash
     8→curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
     9→```
    10→
    11→### What it does
    12→
    13→1. **Foundation** — system upgrade, installs git, base-devel, openssh, github-cli, ansible-core, yay
    14→2. **Secret Unlock** — clones this repo, decrypts `secrets.vault` (one password prompt)
    15→3. **Identity** — deploys SSH keys, sets git config, authenticates `gh`, verifies access
    16→
    17→### Dry run
    18→
    19→Preview what would happen without making changes:
    20→
    21→```bash
    22→curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | DRY_RUN=true bash
    23→```
    24→
    25→### Headless / CI
    26→
    27→```bash
    28→SKOGAI_BECOME_PASSWORD="mypassword" bash bootstrap.sh
    29→```
    30→
    31→## Vault management
    32→
    33→The `secrets.vault` file is an ansible-vault encrypted tar.gz containing SSH keys, a GitHub PAT, and git identity.
    34→
    35→```bash
    36→make help       # show all targets
    37→make decrypt    # vault → secrets/ dir
    38→make encrypt    # secrets/ dir → vault
    39→make edit       # decrypt, open shell, re-encrypt on exit
    40→make verify     # test vault can be decrypted
    41→make lint       # shellcheck bootstrap.sh
    42→make clean      # shred plaintext files
    43→```
    44→
    45→### Vault contents
    46→
    47→```
    48→secrets/
    49→├── ssh/
    50→│   ├── id_ed25519
    51→│   ├── id_ed25519.pub
    52→│   └── config          # optional
    53→├── github_pat           # single line: ghp_...
    54→└── gitconfig            # name=skogix\nemail=...
    55→```
    56→
    57→### Creating the vault from scratch
    58→
    59→```bash
    60→mkdir -p secrets/ssh
    61→cp ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 secrets/ssh/
    62→cp ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub secrets/ssh/
    63→echo "ghp_yourtoken" > secrets/github_pat
    64→printf 'name=skogix\nemail=your@email.com\n' > secrets/gitconfig
    65→make encrypt
    66→```
    67→
    68→## Safety
    69→
    70→- Idempotent: safe to run multiple times
    71→- Cleanup trap: shreds temp files on exit/interrupt
    72→- Arch-only: exits on non-Arch systems
    73→- Non-root: refuses to run as root
    74→- `--needed` flag: skips already-installed packages
    75→- btrfs note: `shred` is ineffective on CoW filesystems
    76→

content.content:
1→SHELL := /bin/bash
     2→VAULT_FILE := secrets.vault
     3→SECRETS_DIR := secrets
     4→ARCHIVE := secrets.tar.gz
     5→
     6→.PHONY: encrypt decrypt edit verify lint clean help
     7→
     8→help: ## Show this help
     9→	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?## .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | \
    10→		awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?## "}; {printf "  \033[36m%-12s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2}'
    11→
    12→encrypt: ## Pack secrets/ → tar.gz → ansible-vault encrypt → secrets.vault
    13→	@if [ ! -d "$(SECRETS_DIR)" ]; then \
    14→		echo "Error: $(SECRETS_DIR)/ directory not found."; \
    15→		echo "Create it with: mkdir -p $(SECRETS_DIR)/ssh"; \
    16→		exit 1; \
    17→	fi
    18→	@echo "Packing $(SECRETS_DIR)/ ..."
    19→	tar czf $(ARCHIVE) -C $(SECRETS_DIR) .
    20→	@echo "Encrypting..."
    21→	ansible-vault encrypt $(ARCHIVE) --output $(VAULT_FILE)
    22→	@rm -f $(ARCHIVE)
    23→	@echo "Created $(VAULT_FILE)"
    24→	@echo "Don't forget to shred plaintext: make clean"
    25→
    26→decrypt: ## Decrypt secrets.vault → tar.gz → secrets/
    27→	@if [ ! -f "$(VAULT_FILE)" ]; then \
    28→		echo "Error: $(VAULT_FILE) not found."; \
    29→		exit 1; \
    30→	fi
    31→	@echo "Decrypting..."
    32→	ansible-vault decrypt $(VAULT_FILE) --output $(ARCHIVE)
    33→	@mkdir -p $(SECRETS_DIR)
    34→	tar xzf $(ARCHIVE) -C $(SECRETS_DIR)
    35→	@rm -f $(ARCHIVE)
    36→	@echo "Extracted to $(SECRETS_DIR)/"
    37→
    38→edit: ## Decrypt, open shell in secrets/, re-encrypt on exit
    39→	@$(MAKE) decrypt
    40→	@echo "Opening shell in $(SECRETS_DIR)/. Exit shell to re-encrypt."
    41→	@cd $(SECRETS_DIR) && $$SHELL || true
    42→	@$(MAKE) encrypt
    43→	@$(MAKE) clean
    44→
    45→verify: ## Test that vault can be decrypted
    46→	@if [ ! -f "$(VAULT_FILE)" ]; then \
    47→		echo "Error: $(VAULT_FILE) not found."; \
    48→		exit 1; \
    49→	fi
    50→	@ansible-vault decrypt $(VAULT_FILE) --output /dev/null && \
    51→		echo "Vault OK: $(VAULT_FILE) can be decrypted." || \
    52→		echo "Vault FAIL: could not decrypt $(VAULT_FILE)."
    53→
    54→lint: ## Run shellcheck on bootstrap.sh
    55→	shellcheck bootstrap.sh
    56→
    57→clean: ## Shred all plaintext secret files
    58→	@if [ -d "$(SECRETS_DIR)" ]; then \
    59→		find $(SECRETS_DIR) -type f -exec shred -u {} \; 2>/dev/null || true; \
    60→		rm -rf $(SECRETS_DIR); \
    61→		echo "Shredded $(SECRETS_DIR)/"; \
    62→	fi
    63→	@if [ -f "$(ARCHIVE)" ]; then \
    64→		shred -u $(ARCHIVE) 2>/dev/null || rm -f $(ARCHIVE); \
    65→		echo "Shredded $(ARCHIVE)"; \
    66→	fi
    67→	@echo "Clean complete."
    68→

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# bootstrap.sh — One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs
     3→# Usage: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
     4→#
     5→# Phases:
     6→#   1. Foundation — install base packages + yay (no auth, no input)
     7→#   2. Secret Unlock — decrypt vault (one password prompt via /dev/tty)
     8→#   3. Identity — deploy SSH keys, git config, gh auth (automatic)
     9→#
    10→# Environment variables:
    11→#   VAULT_PASSWORD  — skip interactive prompt (for CI/headless)
    12→#   DRY_RUN=true    — print what would happen without executing
    13→#   BOOTSTRAP_REPO  — override repo URL (default: SkogAI/bootstrap)
    14→
    15→set -euo pipefail
    16→
    17→# --- Constants ---
    18→BOOTSTRAP_REPO="${BOOTSTRAP_REPO:-https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git}"
    19→WORK_DIR=""
    20→SECRETS_DIR=""
    21→
    22→# --- Colors ---
    23→RED='\033[0;31m'
    24→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    25→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    26→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    27→NC='\033[0m'
    28→
    29→# --- Helpers ---
    30→log()  { printf "${GREEN}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
    31→warn() { printf "${YELLOW}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
    32→err()  { printf "${RED}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
    33→info() { printf "${BLUE}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
    34→
    35→dry_run() { [[ "${DRY_RUN:-}" == "true" ]]; }
    36→
    37→run() {
    38→    if dry_run; then
    39→        info "[DRY_RUN] $*"
    40→    else
    41→        "$@"
    42→    fi
    43→}
    44→
    45→# --- Cleanup trap ---
    46→cleanup() {
    47→    log "Cleaning up..."
    48→    if [[ -n "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" && -d "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" ]]; then
    49→        # shred plaintext secrets
    50→        find "$SECRETS_DIR" -type f -exec shred -u {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
    51→        rm -rf "$SECRETS_DIR"
    52→    fi
    53→    if [[ -n "${WORK_DIR:-}" && -d "${WORK_DIR:-}" ]]; then
    54→        rm -rf "$WORK_DIR"
    55→    fi
    56→    log "Cleanup complete."
    57→}
    58→trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM
    59→
    60→# --- Precondition checks ---
    61→preflight() {
    62→    log "Running preflight checks..."
    63→
    64→    # Must be Arch Linux
    65→    if [[ ! -f /etc/arch-release ]]; then
    66→        err "This script is designed for Arch Linux only."
    67→        exit 1
    68→    fi
    69→
    70→    # Must not be root
    71→    if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
    72→        err "Do not run as root. The script uses sudo where needed."
    73→        exit 1
    74→    fi
    75→
    76→    # Network check
    77→    if ! ping -c1 -W3 archlinux.org &>/dev/null; then
    78→        err "No network connectivity. Check your connection."
    79→        exit 1
    80→    fi
    81→
    82→    log "Preflight checks passed."
    83→}
    84→
    85→# =============================================================================
    86→# Phase 1: Foundation
    87→# =============================================================================
    88→phase_foundation() {
    89→    log "=== Phase 1: Foundation ==="
    90→
    91→    # Full system upgrade
    92→    log "Upgrading system..."
    93→    run sudo pacman -Syu --noconfirm
    94→
    95→    # Install essential packages
    96→    local packages=(
    97→        git
    98→        base-devel
    99→        openssh
   100→        github-cli
   101→        ansible-core
   102→    )
   103→    log "Installing packages: ${packages[*]}"
   104→    run sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm "${packages[@]}"
   105→
   106→    # Install yay if not present
   107→    if command -v yay &>/dev/null; then
   108→        log "yay already installed, skipping."
   109→    else
   110→        log "Building yay from AUR source..."
   111→        if dry_run; then
   112→            info "[DRY_RUN] Would build and install yay"
   113→        else
   114→            local yay_dir
   115→            yay_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
   116→            git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git "$yay_dir/yay"
   117→            (cd "$yay_dir/yay" && makepkg -si --noconfirm)
   118→            rm -rf "$yay_dir"
   119→        fi
   120→    fi
   121→
   122→    log "Phase 1 complete."
   123→}
   124→
   125→# =============================================================================
   126→# Phase 2: Secret Unlock
   127→# =============================================================================
   128→phase_secrets() {
   129→    log "=== Phase 2: Secret Unlock ==="
   130→
   131→    # Clone bootstrap repo (public, HTTPS, no auth needed)
   132→    WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap.XXXXXX)"
   133→    log "Cloning bootstrap repo to $WORK_DIR..."
   134→    if dry_run; then
   135→        info "[DRY_RUN] Would clone $BOOTSTRAP_REPO to $WORK_DIR"
   136→        # Create fake structure for dry run
   137→        mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
   138→        return
   139→    else
   140→        git clone --depth 1 "$BOOTSTRAP_REPO" "$WORK_DIR/repo"
   141→    fi
   142→
   143→    local vault_file="$WORK_DIR/repo/secrets.vault"
   144→    if [[ ! -f "$vault_file" ]]; then
   145→        err "secrets.vault not found in repo!"
   146→        exit 1
   147→    fi
   148→
   149→    # Decrypt vault
   150→    SECRETS_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap-secrets.XXXXXX)"
   151→    local decrypted_archive="$SECRETS_DIR/secrets.tar.gz"
   152→
   153→    if [[ -n "${VAULT_PASSWORD:-}" ]]; then
   154→        log "Using VAULT_PASSWORD environment variable..."
   155→        echo "$VAULT_PASSWORD" | ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
   156→            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
   157→            --vault-password-file /dev/stdin
   158→    else
   159→        log "Prompting for vault password (reads from /dev/tty)..."
   160→        ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
   161→            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
   162→            --vault-password-file <(read -rsp "Vault password: " pw < /dev/tty && echo && echo "$pw")
   163→    fi
   164→
   165→    # Extract
   166→    log "Extracting secrets..."
   167→    tar xzf "$decrypted_archive" -C "$SECRETS_DIR"
   168→    rm -f "$decrypted_archive"
   169→
   170→    # Validate expected files
   171→    local has_key=false
   172→    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
   173→        if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
   174→            has_key=true
   175→            break
   176→        fi
   177→    done
   178→
   179→    if [[ "$has_key" == "false" ]]; then
   180→        err "No SSH private key found in vault! Expected ssh/id_<type>"
   181→        exit 1
   182→    fi
   183→
   184→    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat" ]]; then
   185→        err "github_pat not found in vault!"
   186→        exit 1
   187→    fi
   188→
   189→    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" ]]; then
   190→        err "gitconfig not found in vault!"
   191→        exit 1
   192→    fi
   193→
   194→    log "Phase 2 complete. Secrets decrypted."
   195→}
   196→
   197→# =============================================================================
   198→# Phase 3: Identity
   199→# =============================================================================
   200→phase_identity() {
   201→    log "=== Phase 3: Identity ==="
   202→
   203→    if dry_run; then
   204→        info "[DRY_RUN] Would deploy SSH keys to ~/.ssh/"
   205→        info "[DRY_RUN] Would configure git identity"
   206→        info "[DRY_RUN] Would authenticate gh with PAT"
   207→        info "[DRY_RUN] Would verify SSH and gh access"
   208→        log "Phase 3 complete (dry run)."
   209→        return
   210→    fi
   211→
   212→    # --- SSH keys ---
   213→    log "Deploying SSH keys..."
   214→    mkdir -p ~/.ssh
   215→    chmod 700 ~/.ssh
   216→
   217→    # Find and deploy SSH key files dynamically
   218→    for keyfile in "$SECRETS_DIR"/ssh/id_*; do
   219→        [[ -e "$keyfile" ]] || continue
   220→        local basename
   221→        basename="$(basename "$keyfile")"
   222→        log "  Deploying $basename"
   223→        cp "$keyfile" "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
   224→        if [[ "$basename" == *.pub ]]; then
   225→            chmod 644 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
   226→        else
   227→            chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
   228→        fi
   229→    done
   230→
   231→    # Deploy SSH config if present
   232→    if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" ]]; then
   233→        log "  Deploying ssh config"
   234→        cp "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config"
   235→        chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/config"
   236→    fi
   237→
   238→    # Start ssh-agent and add key
   239→    log "Starting ssh-agent..."
   240→    eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
   241→    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
   242→        if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
   243→            ssh-add "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}"
   244→            break
   245→        fi
   246→    done
   247→
   248→    # --- Git config ---
   249→    log "Configuring git identity..."
   250→    local git_name git_email
   251→    git_name="$(grep '^name=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"
   252→    git_email="$(grep '^email=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"
   253→
   254→    if [[ -n "$git_name" ]]; then
   255→        git config --global user.name "$git_name"
   256→        log "  user.name = $git_name"
   257→    fi
   258→    if [[ -n "$git_email" ]]; then
   259→        git config --global user.email "$git_email"
   260→        log "  user.email = $git_email"
   261→    fi
   262→
   263→    # --- GitHub CLI auth ---
   264→    log "Authenticating GitHub CLI..."
   265→    gh auth login --with-token < "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat"
   266→    gh config set git_protocol ssh
   267→
   268→    # --- Verification ---
   269→    log "Verifying access..."
   270→
   271→    log "  SSH:"
   272→    ssh -T git@github.com 2>&1 || true  # github returns exit 1 even on success
   273→
   274→    log "  GitHub CLI:"
   275→    gh auth status
   276→
   277→    log "Phase 3 complete."
   278→}
   279→
   280→# =============================================================================
   281→# Main
   282→# =============================================================================
   283→main() {
   284→    echo ""
   285→    log "SkogAI Bootstrap"
   286→    log "================"
   287→    echo ""
   288→
   289→    if dry_run; then
   290→        warn "DRY_RUN mode enabled — no changes will be made."
   291→        echo ""
   292→    fi
   293→
   294→    preflight
   295→    phase_foundation
   296→    phase_secrets
   297→    phase_identity
   298→
   299→    echo ""
   300→    log "Bootstrap complete!"
   301→    log "Next steps:"
   302→    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/claude ~/claude"
   303→    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/skogansible ~/.ansible"
   304→    echo ""
   305→}
   306→
   307→main "$@"
   308→

content.text:
Excellent! I have thoroughly explored the codebase. Here's my comprehensive analysis:

## Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. Project Purpose

**Bootstrap** is a one-liner bootstrap system for fresh Arch Linux installations. It automates the complete setup from a bare Arch Linux system to fully configured with SSH identity, Git configuration, and GitHub CLI authentication.

The core idea: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash`

### 2. File Structure

```
/home/skogix/bootstrap/
├── bootstrap.sh              # Main executable bootstrap script (297 lines)
├── Makefile                  # Vault management and build targets
├── README.md                 # Project documentation
├── .gitignore               # Git ignore rules (plaintext secrets, tar.gz, editors)
├── secrets.vault            # Ansible-vault encrypted tar.gz (3.1K, AES256)
├── .git/                    # Git repository metadata
└── secrets/                 # Decrypted secrets directory (CURRENT STATE)
    ├── ssh/
    │   ├── id_ed25519       # SSH private key
    │   └── id_ed25519.pub   # SSH public key
    ├── gitconfig            # Git name/email configuration
    └── github_pat           # GitHub Personal Access Token
```

### 3. Build/Test/Lint Commands

**Makefile targets** (defined in `/home/skogix/bootstrap/Makefile`):

| Target | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `make help` | Show all targets |
| `make encrypt` | Pack `secrets/` → tar.gz → ansible-vault → `secrets.vault` |
| `make decrypt` | Decrypt `secrets.vault` → tar.gz → `secrets/` |
| `make edit` | Decrypt, open shell in secrets/, re-encrypt on exit |
| `make verify` | Test that vault can be decrypted |
| `make lint` | Run `shellcheck` on bootstrap.sh |
| `make clean` | Shred all plaintext secret files |

**Note**: Shellcheck is not currently installed in the environment.

### 4. Architecture & Key Patterns

**Three-Phase Bootstrap Design** (in `bootstrap.sh`):

**Phase 1: Foundation** (~40 lines)
- System upgrade: `sudo pacman -Syu --noconfirm`
- Install essential packages: git, base-devel, openssh, github-cli, ansible-core
- Build and install yay (AUR helper) if needed

**Phase 2: Secret Unlock** (~70 lines)
- Clones the bootstrap repo (public, HTTPS, no auth)
- Decrypts vault using ansible-vault with password prompt
- Extracts tar.gz containing SSH keys, GitHub PAT, and gitconfig
- Validates all required secrets are present

**Phase 3: Identity** (~80 lines)
- Deploys SSH keys to `~/.ssh/` with proper permissions
- Configures git identity (user.name, user.email)
- Authenticates GitHub CLI with PAT
- Verifies SSH and gh access

**Key Patterns**:
- **Idempotent**: Safe to run multiple times
- **Error handling**: `set -euo pipefail` + extensive precondition checks
- **Cleanup trap**: Auto-shreds temp files on exit/interrupt
- **Dry-run support**: `DRY_RUN=true` environment variable
- **CI-friendly**: `VAULT_PASSWORD` env var for headless/CI execution
- **Platform-specific**: Only runs on Arch Linux (`/etc/arch-release` check)
- **Non-root**: Refuses to run as root (uses `sudo` where needed)
- **Colored output**: ANSI color codes for log/warn/err/info functions

**Shell Helpers** (lines 30-43):
```bash
log()    # Green [bootstrap]
warn()   # Yellow [bootstrap]
err()    # Red [bootstrap]
info()   # Blue [bootstrap]
dry_run() # Check DRY_RUN env var
run()     # Execute or dry-print
```

### 5. Configuration & Instruction Files

**No cursor rules or CLAUDE.md files found.** The project is purely:
- A shell script (bash)
- Makefile for vault operations
- Git repository

### 6. Key Configuration Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.gitignore` | Ignores plaintext secrets, tar.gz, editor temp files |
| `.git/config` | Remote: `https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git`, branch: master |
| `Makefile` | Vault encryption/decryption/validation automation |
| `README.md` | Full usage documentation |

### 7. Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `VAULT_PASSWORD` | Skip interactive prompt (for CI/headless) | None (prompts `/dev/tty`) |
| `DRY_RUN` | Preview without executing | None |
| `BOOTSTRAP_REPO` | Override repo URL | `https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git` |

### 8. Current Repository State

- **Branch**: master (tracking origin/master)
- **Remote**: https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git
- **Recent commits**:
  - `87d3b07` - skogix test
  - `e7a0647` - Update vault with real GitHub PAT
  - `ce30dc9` - Fix raw URL: use master branch, not main
  - `8d1fee2` - Add bootstrap system: one-liner Arch Linux setup
  - `67df232` - Initial commit

- **Current state**: Secrets are currently decrypted (plaintext directory exists)
  - This is safe for the current development context (verified by `.gitignore`)

### 9. Dependencies

**Runtime**:
- ansible-core (for ansible-vault)
- git
- bash
- pacman (Arch package manager)
- makepkg (for yay builds)
- ssh-agent
- gh (GitHub CLI)

**Development**:
- shellcheck (for linting - not installed)
- make

### 10. Safety & Security Notes

- **CoW filesystems**: `shred` is ineffective on Btrfs (noted in README)
- **Vault**: Uses Ansible Vault with AES256 encryption
- **Idempotent**: Multiple runs are safe
- **Cleanup**: Automatic `trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM` removes temp files
- **Permissions**: SSH keys deployed with proper 600 (private) / 644 (public) permissions

---

**Summary**: This is a minimal, well-structured bash-based bootstrap automation tool designed for Arch Linux fresh installations. It follows shell best practices (strict mode, error handling, cleanup traps, dry-run support) and provides a secure, idempotent way to configure SSH, Git, and GitHub authentication from a single command.

Excellent! I have thoroughly explored the codebase. Here's my comprehensive analysis:

## Codebase Exploration Report

### 1. Project Purpose

**Bootstrap** is a one-liner bootstrap system for fresh Arch Linux installations. It automates the complete setup from a bare Arch Linux system to fully configured with SSH identity, Git configuration, and GitHub CLI authentication.

The core idea: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash`

### 2. File Structure

```
/home/skogix/bootstrap/
├── bootstrap.sh              # Main executable bootstrap script (297 lines)
├── Makefile                  # Vault management and build targets
├── README.md                 # Project documentation
├── .gitignore               # Git ignore rules (plaintext secrets, tar.gz, editors)
├── secrets.vault            # Ansible-vault encrypted tar.gz (3.1K, AES256)
├── .git/                    # Git repository metadata
└── secrets/                 # Decrypted secrets directory (CURRENT STATE)
    ├── ssh/
    │   ├── id_ed25519       # SSH private key
    │   └── id_ed25519.pub   # SSH public key
    ├── gitconfig            # Git name/email configuration
    └── github_pat           # GitHub Personal Access Token
```

### 3. Build/Test/Lint Commands

**Makefile targets** (defined in `/home/skogix/bootstrap/Makefile`):

| Target | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `make help` | Show all targets |
| `make encrypt` | Pack `secrets/` → tar.gz → ansible-vault → `secrets.vault` |
| `make decrypt` | Decrypt `secrets.vault` → tar.gz → `secrets/` |
| `make edit` | Decrypt, open shell in secrets/, re-encrypt on exit |
| `make verify` | Test that vault can be decrypted |
| `make lint` | Run `shellcheck` on bootstrap.sh |
| `make clean` | Shred all plaintext secret files |

**Note**: Shellcheck is not currently installed in the environment.

### 4. Architecture & Key Patterns

**Three-Phase Bootstrap Design** (in `bootstrap.sh`):

**Phase 1: Foundation** (~40 lines)
- System upgrade: `sudo pacman -Syu --noconfirm`
- Install essential packages: git, base-devel, openssh, github-cli, ansible-core
- Build and install yay (AUR helper) if needed

**Phase 2: Secret Unlock** (~70 lines)
- Clones the bootstrap repo (public, HTTPS, no auth)
- Decrypts vault using ansible-vault with password prompt
- Extracts tar.gz containing SSH keys, GitHub PAT, and gitconfig
- Validates all required secrets are present

**Phase 3: Identity** (~80 lines)
- Deploys SSH keys to `~/.ssh/` with proper permissions
- Configures git identity (user.name, user.email)
- Authenticates GitHub CLI with PAT
- Verifies SSH and gh access

**Key Patterns**:
- **Idempotent**: Safe to run multiple times
- **Error handling**: `set -euo pipefail` + extensive precondition checks
- **Cleanup trap**: Auto-shreds temp files on exit/interrupt
- **Dry-run support**: `DRY_RUN=true` environment variable
- **CI-friendly**: `VAULT_PASSWORD` env var for headless/CI execution
- **Platform-specific**: Only runs on Arch Linux (`/etc/arch-release` check)
- **Non-root**: Refuses to run as root (uses `sudo` where needed)
- **Colored output**: ANSI color codes for log/warn/err/info functions

**Shell Helpers** (lines 30-43):
```bash
log()    # Green [bootstrap]
warn()   # Yellow [bootstrap]
err()    # Red [bootstrap]
info()   # Blue [bootstrap]
dry_run() # Check DRY_RUN env var
run()     # Execute or dry-print
```

### 5. Configuration & Instruction Files

**No cursor rules or CLAUDE.md files found.** The project is purely:
- A shell script (bash)
- Makefile for vault operations
- Git repository

### 6. Key Configuration Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.gitignore` | Ignores plaintext secrets, tar.gz, editor temp files |
| `.git/config` | Remote: `https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git`, branch: master |
| `Makefile` | Vault encryption/decryption/validation automation |
| `README.md` | Full usage documentation |

### 7. Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `VAULT_PASSWORD` | Skip interactive prompt (for CI/headless) | None (prompts `/dev/tty`) |
| `DRY_RUN` | Preview without executing | None |
| `BOOTSTRAP_REPO` | Override repo URL | `https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git` |

### 8. Current Repository State

- **Branch**: master (tracking origin/master)
- **Remote**: https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git
- **Recent commits**:
  - `87d3b07` - skogix test
  - `e7a0647` - Update vault with real GitHub PAT
  - `ce30dc9` - Fix raw URL: use master branch, not main
  - `8d1fee2` - Add bootstrap system: one-liner Arch Linux setup
  - `67df232` - Initial commit

- **Current state**: Secrets are currently decrypted (plaintext directory exists)
  - This is safe for the current development context (verified by `.gitignore`)

### 9. Dependencies

**Runtime**:
- ansible-core (for ansible-vault)
- git
- bash
- pacman (Arch package manager)
- makepkg (for yay builds)
- ssh-agent
- gh (GitHub CLI)

**Development**:
- shellcheck (for linting - not installed)
- make

### 10. Safety & Security Notes

- **CoW filesystems**: `shred` is ineffective on Btrfs (noted in README)
- **Vault**: Uses Ansible Vault with AES256 encryption
- **Idempotent**: Multiple runs are safe
- **Cleanup**: Automatic `trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM` removes temp files
- **Permissions**: SSH keys deployed with proper 600 (private) / 644 (public) permissions

---

**Summary**: This is a minimal, well-structured bash-based bootstrap automation tool designed for Arch Linux fresh installations. It follows shell best practices (strict mode, error handling, cleanup traps, dry-run support) and provides a secure, idempotent way to configure SSH, Git, and GitHub authentication from a single command.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

One-liner bootstrap system for fresh Arch Linux installations. Automates setup from bare Arch to fully configured with SSH identity, Git config, and GitHub CLI auth via an Ansible Vault-encrypted secrets bundle.

## Key Commands

```bash
make lint        # Run shellcheck on bootstrap.sh
make encrypt     # Pack secrets/ → tar.gz → ansible-vault → secrets.vault
make decrypt     # Decrypt secrets.vault → tar.gz → secrets/
make edit        # Decrypt, open shell in secrets/, re-encrypt on exit
make verify      # Test that vault can be decrypted
make clean       # Shred all plaintext secret files
```

## Architecture

**bootstrap.sh** is the single entry point, run via curl pipe. Three sequential phases:

1. **Foundation** — `pacman -Syu`, install core packages (git, openssh, github-cli, ansible-core), build yay AUR helper
2. **Secret Unlock** — Clone this repo via HTTPS, decrypt `secrets.vault` with ansible-vault, extract SSH keys + GitHub PAT + gitconfig
3. **Identity** — Deploy SSH keys (`~/.ssh/`), configure git user, authenticate `gh` CLI, verify connectivity

**Key design properties**: idempotent (safe to re-run), `set -euo pipefail` strict mode, cleanup trap that shreds temp files on exit/interrupt, Arch-only (`/etc/arch-release` check), refuses to run as root.

## Environment Variables

- `VAULT_PASSWORD` — Skip interactive vault password prompt (for CI/headless)
- `DRY_RUN=true` — Preview commands without executing
- `BOOTSTRAP_REPO` — Override the default repo URL

## Secrets Layout (inside vault)

```
secrets/
├── ssh/id_ed25519      # SSH private key
├── ssh/id_ed25519.pub  # SSH public key
├── gitconfig           # Git name/email
└── github_pat          # GitHub PAT
```

Plaintext secrets are gitignored. `shred` is used for cleanup but is ineffective on CoW filesystems (Btrfs).

    58→trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM
    59→
    60→# --- Precondition checks ---
    61→preflight() {
    62→    log "Running preflight checks..."
    63→
    64→    # Must be Arch Linux
    65→    if [[ ! -f /etc/arch-release ]]; then
    66→        err "This script is designed for Arch Linux only."

    98→        base-devel

   225→            chmod 644 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
   226→        else
   227→            chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
   228→        fi
   229→    done
   230→
   231→    # Deploy SSH config if present
   232→    if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" ]]; then
   233→        log "  Deploying ssh config"
   234→        cp "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config"

   263→    # --- GitHub CLI auth ---

   266→    gh config set git_protocol ssh

   273→

   277→    log "Phase 3 complete."
   278→}
   279→
   280→# =============================================================================
   281→# Main
   282→# =============================================================================
   283→main() {
   284→    echo ""
   285→    log "SkogAI Bootstrap"
   286→    log "================"
   287→    echo ""
   288→

   296→    phase_secrets
   297→    phase_identity
   298→
   299→    echo ""
   300→    log "Bootstrap complete!"

   302→    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/claude ~/claude"
   303→    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/skogansible ~/.ansible"

   305→}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bootstrap.sh — One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs
# Usage: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
#
# Phases:
#   1. Foundation — install base packages + yay (no auth, no input)
#   2. Secret Unlock — decrypt vault (one password prompt via /dev/tty)
#   3. Identity — deploy SSH keys, git config, gh auth (automatic)
#
# Environment variables:
#   VAULT_PASSWORD  — skip interactive prompt (for CI/headless)
#   DRY_RUN=true    — print what would happen without executing
#   BOOTSTRAP_REPO  — override repo URL (default: SkogAI/bootstrap)

set -euo pipefail

# --- Constants ---
BOOTSTRAP_REPO="${BOOTSTRAP_REPO:-https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git}"
WORK_DIR=""
SECRETS_DIR=""

# --- Colors ---
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

# --- Helpers ---
log()  { printf "${GREEN}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
warn() { printf "${YELLOW}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
err()  { printf "${RED}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
info() { printf "${BLUE}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }

dry_run() { [[ "${DRY_RUN:-}" == "true" ]]; }

run() {
    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] $*"
    else
        "$@"
    fi
}

# --- Cleanup trap ---
cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up..."
    if [[ -n "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" && -d "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" ]]; then
        # shred plaintext secrets
        find "$SECRETS_DIR" -type f -exec shred -u {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
        rm -rf "$SECRETS_DIR"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${WORK_DIR:-}" && -d "${WORK_DIR:-}" ]]; then
        rm -rf "$WORK_DIR"
    fi
    log "Cleanup complete."
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# --- Precondition checks ---
preflight() {
    log "Running preflight checks..."

    # Must be Arch Linux
    if [[ ! -f /etc/arch-release ]]; then
        err "This script is designed for Arch Linux only."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Must not be root
    if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
        err "Do not run as root. The script uses sudo where needed."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Network check
    if ! ping -c1 -W3 archlinux.org &>/dev/null; then
        err "No network connectivity. Check your connection."
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Preflight checks passed."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 1: Foundation
# =============================================================================
phase_foundation() {
    log "=== Phase 1: Foundation ==="

    # Full system upgrade
    log "Upgrading system..."
    run sudo pacman -Syu --noconfirm

    # Install essential packages
    local packages=(
        git
        base-devel
        openssh
        github-cli
        ansible-core
    )
    log "Installing packages: ${packages[*]}"
    run sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm "${packages[@]}"

    # Install yay if not present
    if command -v yay &>/dev/null; then
        log "yay already installed, skipping."
    else
        log "Building yay from AUR source..."
        if dry_run; then
            info "[DRY_RUN] Would build and install yay"
        else
            local yay_dir
            yay_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
            git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git "$yay_dir/yay"
            (cd "$yay_dir/yay" && makepkg -si --noconfirm)
            rm -rf "$yay_dir"
        fi
    fi

    log "Phase 1 complete."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 2: Secret Unlock
# =============================================================================
phase_secrets() {
    log "=== Phase 2: Secret Unlock ==="

    # Clone bootstrap repo (public, HTTPS, no auth needed)
    WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap.XXXXXX)"
    log "Cloning bootstrap repo to $WORK_DIR..."
    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would clone $BOOTSTRAP_REPO to $WORK_DIR"
        # Create fake structure for dry run
        mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
        return
    else
        git clone --depth 1 "$BOOTSTRAP_REPO" "$WORK_DIR/repo"
    fi

    local vault_file="$WORK_DIR/repo/secrets.vault"
    if [[ ! -f "$vault_file" ]]; then
        err "secrets.vault not found in repo!"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Decrypt vault
    SECRETS_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap-secrets.XXXXXX)"
    local decrypted_archive="$SECRETS_DIR/secrets.tar.gz"

    if [[ -n "${VAULT_PASSWORD:-}" ]]; then
        log "Using VAULT_PASSWORD environment variable..."
        echo "$VAULT_PASSWORD" | ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
            --vault-password-file /dev/stdin
    else
        log "Prompting for vault password (reads from /dev/tty)..."
        ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
            --vault-password-file <(read -rsp "Vault password: " pw < /dev/tty && echo && echo "$pw")
    fi

    # Extract
    log "Extracting secrets..."
    tar xzf "$decrypted_archive" -C "$SECRETS_DIR"
    rm -f "$decrypted_archive"

    # Validate expected files
    local has_key=false
    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
        if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
            has_key=true
            break
        fi
    done

    if [[ "$has_key" == "false" ]]; then
        err "No SSH private key found in vault! Expected ssh/id_<type>"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat" ]]; then
        err "github_pat not found in vault!"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" ]]; then
        err "gitconfig not found in vault!"
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Phase 2 complete. Secrets decrypted."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 3: Identity
# =============================================================================
phase_identity() {
    log "=== Phase 3: Identity ==="

    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would deploy SSH keys to ~/.ssh/"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would configure git identity"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would authenticate gh with PAT"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would verify SSH and gh access"
        log "Phase 3 complete (dry run)."
        return
    fi

    # --- SSH keys ---
    log "Deploying SSH keys..."
    mkdir -p ~/.ssh
    chmod 700 ~/.ssh

    # Find and deploy SSH key files dynamically
    for keyfile in "$SECRETS_DIR"/ssh/id_*; do
        [[ -e "$keyfile" ]] || continue
        local basename
        basename="$(basename "$keyfile")"
        log "  Deploying $basename"
        cp "$keyfile" "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        if [[ "$basename" == *.pub ]]; then
            chmod 644 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        else
            chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        fi
    done

    # Deploy SSH config if present
    if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" ]]; then
        log "  Deploying ssh config"
        cp "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config"
        chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/config"
    fi

    # Start ssh-agent and add key
    log "Starting ssh-agent..."
    eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
        if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
            ssh-add "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}"
            break
        fi
    done

    # --- Git config ---
    log "Configuring git identity..."
    local git_name git_email
    git_name="$(grep '^name=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"
    git_email="$(grep '^email=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"

    if [[ -n "$git_name" ]]; then
        git config --global user.name "$git_name"
        log "  user.name = $git_name"
    fi
    if [[ -n "$git_email" ]]; then
        git config --global user.email "$git_email"
        log "  user.email = $git_email"
    fi

    # --- GitHub CLI auth ---
    log "Authenticating GitHub CLI..."
    gh auth login --with-token < "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat"
    gh config set git_protocol ssh

    # --- Verification ---
    log "Verifying access..."

    log "  SSH:"
    ssh -T git@github.com 2>&1 || true  # github returns exit 1 even on success

    log "  GitHub CLI:"
    gh auth status

    log "Phase 3 complete."
}

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================
main() {
    echo ""
    log "SkogAI Bootstrap"
    log "================"
    echo ""

    if dry_run; then
        warn "DRY_RUN mode enabled — no changes will be made."
        echo ""
    fi

    preflight
    phase_foundation
    phase_secrets
    phase_identity

    echo ""
    log "Bootstrap complete!"
    log "Next steps:"
    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/claude ~/claude"
    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/skogansible ~/.ansible"
    echo ""
}

main "$@"

content.thinking:
Now I can see the full picture. Let me trace through what happened:

1. Lines 1-21: User was already setting up manually before bootstrap — installing bitwarden, kitty, chezmoi, etc. The bootstrap was NOT the first thing they ran.

2. Line 22: `curl -fsSL ... | bash` — bootstrap ran

3. Lines 23-25: `ls`, `ls -la`, `ls -la .ssh` — checking results. The key issue: the cleanup trap!

**The big bug**: The `trap cleanup EXIT` at line 58 of bootstrap.sh runs on EXIT. When the script finishes (line 307), it triggers cleanup which **shreds the decrypted secrets AND removes WORK_DIR**. But wait — does it shred the keys that were already copied to ~/.ssh/? No, it only shreds SECRETS_DIR (the temp dir). So the keys SHOULD be in ~/.ssh/ after bootstrap.


But looking at the history, right after bootstrap finishes the user checks `.ssh` and then immediately tries `gh` which fails, prompting three auth login attempts. Let me look at what the cleanup function actually does — it's shredding files in the temporary secrets directory and removing that directory, but I need to see if there's something else happening that might affect the SSH keys or GitHub authentication setup.

The script gets piped through curl directly to bash, so stdin is the curl stream. The vault password prompt uses `/dev/tty` which should still work even when piped, and the GitHub token gets read from a file rather than stdin, so that should be fine too. But I'm wondering if there's an issue with how the TTY is being handled when the script runs this way.

Actually, I think I found the real problem. The Makefile encrypts the secrets directory with `tar czf secrets.tar.gz secrets/`, which means the tar archive contains `secrets/` as a top-level directory. When the script extracts this to `$SECRETS_DIR`, the files end up nested one level deeper than expected — at `$SECRETS_DIR/secrets/ssh/id_ed25519` instead of `$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/id_ed25519`. But the script is looking for them at the shallower path, so it won't find the keys.

content.text:
Found it. **The tar paths don't match.**

**Makefile** (line 19) packs with:
```bash
tar czf secrets.tar.gz -C secrets .
```
This creates a tar with paths like `./ssh/id_ed25519`, `./github_pat`, etc.

**bootstrap.sh** (line 167) extracts with:
```bash
tar xzf "$decrypted_archive" -C "$SECRETS_DIR"
```
This extracts to `$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/id_ed25519` — so the paths **do** actually align. That's not the bug then.

The real problem is more fundamental: **the cleanup trap destroys everything on EXIT**. Look at the flow:

1. Phase 3 deploys keys to `~/.ssh/`, runs `gh auth login --with-token` — this should work
2. Script ends, `main` returns
3. **`trap cleanup EXIT` fires** — shreds `$SECRETS_DIR` (fine, those are temps)

So the keys *should* survive in `~/.ssh/`... Let me reconsider. The `gh auth login --with-token` on line 265 — when the script is run via **`curl | bash`**, there could be stdin issues. And the `ssh-agent` started on line 240 dies with the script since it's in a subprocess.

Here's what I think actually went wrong:

1. **`gh auth login --with-token` likely failed silently** — the PAT file might have had a trailing newline or the token was bad/expired. The user had to `gh auth login` manually 3 times (lines 28-30, 95, 99).

2. **`ssh-agent` was useless** — the `eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"` on line 240 starts an agent in the script's process. When the script exits, that agent is gone. The keys are in `~/.ssh/` but no agent persists.

3. **No "next step" tooling was installed** — the user immediately needed `chezmoi`, `kitty`, etc. (lines 31, 35) which bootstrap doesn't cover. The script gets you identity but not a usable workstation.

4. **The script was run *after* manual setup** — lines 1-21 show the user was already installing things manually before running bootstrap. By the time bootstrap ran, some state was already partially configured, possibly conflicting.

**Bottom line:** The script technically works for deploying files, but the *useful outcome* — a working `gh` auth and persistent SSH agent — didn't survive the script exiting. And it stops too early; identity alone isn't enough to actually *use* the machine. Want to fix these issues?

so this is obviously 99% useless and not needed. please talk me through why this would not work: use pacman to install uv -> uv tool install ansible -> ansible-vault decrypt on a encrypted ssh key/secret of some kind -> use that key to gh repo clone skogix/secrets to ~/.ssh ?

content.text:
That flow is much simpler and would work. Let me trace through it:

1. `pacman -S uv` — installs uv
2. `uv tool install ansible` — gets ansible-vault without polluting system python
3. `ansible-vault decrypt secrets.vault` — decrypt the vault (one password prompt)
4. Extract SSH key + PAT from vault
5. `gh auth login --with-token` with the PAT
6. `gh repo clone skogix/secrets ~/.ssh` — pull full secrets repo

That's basically it. The only thing I'd flag:

**Step 5 needs to happen before step 6** — `gh repo clone` requires auth. So you still need the PAT in the vault, or you clone via HTTPS public repo first. But looking at your history (line 78), `skogix/secrets` is a private repo — so you need auth first.

The minimal chain is really:

```
pacman -S uv github-cli
uv tool install ansible-core
ansible-vault decrypt secrets.vault  # → get PAT + maybe a bootstrap SSH key
gh auth login --with-token < pat
gh repo clone skogix/secrets ~/.ssh
```

The current `bootstrap.sh` overengineers this into 300 lines with phases, color logging, yay building, ssh-agent management, git config parsing, etc. — when all you actually need is those ~6 commands.

**One question though:** how does the script get `secrets.vault` in the first place? Currently it `git clone`s this bootstrap repo (public). You'd still need that step, or host the vault file somewhere curl-able. The raw GitHub URL already works for the script itself, so you could just `curl` the vault file directly too.

message.content: so we would have two things encrypted, a pat and a key of some kind?

content.text:
Right, you could simplify to just **one encrypted thing: the PAT**.

The PAT is enough to `gh auth login`, then `gh repo clone skogix/secrets` pulls down everything else (SSH keys, gitconfig, whatever). No need to encrypt SSH keys separately in the vault.

So the whole chain becomes:

```
pacman -S uv github-cli
uv tool install ansible-core
curl -fsSL <raw-vault-url> -o pat.vault
ansible-vault decrypt pat.vault    # → plaintext PAT
gh auth login --with-token < pat.vault
gh repo clone skogix/secrets ~/.ssh
```

One password prompt, one encrypted secret, done.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bootstrap.sh — One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs
# Usage: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
#
# Phases:
#   1. Foundation — install base packages + yay (no auth, no input)
#   2. Secret Unlock — decrypt vault (one password prompt via /dev/tty)
#   3. Identity — deploy SSH keys, git config, gh auth (automatic)
#
# Environment variables:
#   VAULT_PASSWORD  — skip interactive prompt (for CI/headless)
#   DRY_RUN=true    — print what would happen without executing
#   BOOTSTRAP_REPO  — override repo URL (default: SkogAI/bootstrap)

set -euo pipefail

# --- Constants ---
BOOTSTRAP_REPO="${BOOTSTRAP_REPO:-https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git}"
WORK_DIR=""
SECRETS_DIR=""

# --- Colors ---
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

# --- Helpers ---
log()  { printf "${GREEN}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
warn() { printf "${YELLOW}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
err()  { printf "${RED}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
info() { printf "${BLUE}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }

dry_run() { [[ "${DRY_RUN:-}" == "true" ]]; }

run() {
    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] $*"
    else
        "$@"
    fi
}

# --- Cleanup trap ---
cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up..."
    if [[ -n "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" && -d "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" ]]; then
        # shred plaintext secrets
        find "$SECRETS_DIR" -type f -exec shred -u {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
        rm -rf "$SECRETS_DIR"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${WORK_DIR:-}" && -d "${WORK_DIR:-}" ]]; then
        rm -rf "$WORK_DIR"
    fi
    log "Cleanup complete."
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# --- Precondition checks ---
preflight() {
    log "Running preflight checks..."

    # Must be Arch Linux
    if [[ ! -f /etc/arch-release ]]; then
        err "This script is designed for Arch Linux only."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Must not be root
    if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
        err "Do not run as root. The script uses sudo where needed."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Network check
    if ! ping -c1 -W3 archlinux.org &>/dev/null; then
        err "No network connectivity. Check your connection."
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Preflight checks passed."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 1: Foundation
# =============================================================================
phase_foundation() {
    log "=== Phase 1: Foundation ==="

    # Full system upgrade
    log "Upgrading system..."
    run sudo pacman -Syu --noconfirm

    # Install essential packages
    local packages=(
        git
        base-devel
        openssh
        github-cli
        ansible-core
    )
    log "Installing packages: ${packages[*]}"
    run sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm "${packages[@]}"

    # Install yay if not present
    if command -v yay &>/dev/null; then
        log "yay already installed, skipping."
    else
        log "Building yay from AUR source..."
        if dry_run; then
            info "[DRY_RUN] Would build and install yay"
        else
            local yay_dir
            yay_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
            git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git "$yay_dir/yay"
            (cd "$yay_dir/yay" && makepkg -si --noconfirm)
            rm -rf "$yay_dir"
        fi
    fi

    log "Phase 1 complete."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 2: Secret Unlock
# =============================================================================
phase_secrets() {
    log "=== Phase 2: Secret Unlock ==="

    # Clone bootstrap repo (public, HTTPS, no auth needed)
    WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap.XXXXXX)"
    log "Cloning bootstrap repo to $WORK_DIR..."
    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would clone $BOOTSTRAP_REPO to $WORK_DIR"
        # Create fake structure for dry run
        mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
        return
    else
        git clone --depth 1 "$BOOTSTRAP_REPO" "$WORK_DIR/repo"
    fi

    local vault_file="$WORK_DIR/repo/secrets.vault"
    if [[ ! -f "$vault_file" ]]; then
        err "secrets.vault not found in repo!"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Decrypt vault
    SECRETS_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap-secrets.XXXXXX)"
    local decrypted_archive="$SECRETS_DIR/secrets.tar.gz"

    if [[ -n "${VAULT_PASSWORD:-}" ]]; then
        log "Using VAULT_PASSWORD environment variable..."
        echo "$VAULT_PASSWORD" | ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
            --vault-password-file /dev/stdin
    else
        log "Prompting for vault password (reads from /dev/tty)..."
        ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
            --vault-password-file <(read -rsp "Vault password: " pw < /dev/tty && echo && echo "$pw")
    fi

    # Extract
    log "Extracting secrets..."
    tar xzf "$decrypted_archive" -C "$SECRETS_DIR"
    rm -f "$decrypted_archive"

    # Validate expected files
    local has_key=false
    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
        if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
            has_key=true
            break
        fi
    done

    if [[ "$has_key" == "false" ]]; then
        err "No SSH private key found in vault! Expected ssh/id_<type>"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat" ]]; then
        err "github_pat not found in vault!"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" ]]; then
        err "gitconfig not found in vault!"
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Phase 2 complete. Secrets decrypted."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 3: Identity
# =============================================================================
phase_identity() {
    log "=== Phase 3: Identity ==="

    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would deploy SSH keys to ~/.ssh/"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would configure git identity"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would authenticate gh with PAT"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would verify SSH and gh access"
        log "Phase 3 complete (dry run)."
        return
    fi

    # --- SSH keys ---
    log "Deploying SSH keys..."
    mkdir -p ~/.ssh
    chmod 700 ~/.ssh

    # Find and deploy SSH key files dynamically
    for keyfile in "$SECRETS_DIR"/ssh/id_*; do
        [[ -e "$keyfile" ]] || continue
        local basename
        basename="$(basename "$keyfile")"
        log "  Deploying $basename"
        cp "$keyfile" "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        if [[ "$basename" == *.pub ]]; then
            chmod 644 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        else
            chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        fi
    done

    # Deploy SSH config if present
    if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" ]]; then
        log "  Deploying ssh config"
        cp "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config"
        chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/config"
    fi

    # Start ssh-agent and add key
    log "Starting ssh-agent..."
    eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
        if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
            ssh-add "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}"
            break
        fi
    done

    # --- Git config ---
    log "Configuring git identity..."
    local git_name git_email
    git_name="$(grep '^name=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"
    git_email="$(grep '^email=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"

    if [[ -n "$git_name" ]]; then
        git config --global user.name "$git_name"
        log "  user.name = $git_name"
    fi
    if [[ -n "$git_email" ]]; then
        git config --global user.email "$git_email"
        log "  user.email = $git_email"
    fi

    # --- GitHub CLI auth ---
    log "Authenticating GitHub CLI..."
    gh auth login --with-token < "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat"
    gh config set git_protocol ssh

    # --- Verification ---
    log "Verifying access..."

    log "  SSH:"
    ssh -T git@github.com 2>&1 || true  # github returns exit 1 even on success

    log "  GitHub CLI:"
    gh auth status

    log "Phase 3 complete."
}

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================
main() {
    echo ""
    log "SkogAI Bootstrap"
    log "================"
    echo ""

    if dry_run; then
        warn "DRY_RUN mode enabled — no changes will be made."
        echo ""
    fi

    preflight
    phase_foundation
    phase_secrets
    phase_identity

    echo ""
    log "Bootstrap complete!"
    log "Next steps:"
    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/claude ~/claude"
    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/skogansible ~/.ansible"
    echo ""
}

main "$@"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bootstrap.sh — One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux
# curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
set -euo pipefail

VAULT_URL="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/pat.vault"
SECRETS_REPO="skogix/secrets"

err() { printf '\033[0;31m[!]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
log() { printf '\033[0;32m[+]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }

cleanup() { rm -f /tmp/pat.vault /tmp/pat; }
trap cleanup EXIT

# Preflight
[[ -f /etc/arch-release ]] || { err "Arch Linux only"; exit 1; }
[[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]         || { err "Don't run as root"; exit 1; }

# Install deps
log "Installing packages..."
sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm github-cli uv ansible-core > /dev/null

# Decrypt PAT
log "Fetching vault..."
curl -fsSL "$VAULT_URL" -o /tmp/pat.vault

if [[ -n "${VAULT_PASSWORD:-}" ]]; then
    echo "$VAULT_PASSWORD" | ansible-vault decrypt /tmp/pat.vault \
        --output /tmp/pat --vault-password-file /dev/stdin
else
    ansible-vault decrypt /tmp/pat.vault --output /tmp/pat \
        --vault-password-file <(read -rsp "Vault password: " pw < /dev/tty && echo && echo "$pw")
fi

# Auth and clone secrets
log "Authenticating gh..."
gh auth login --with-token < /tmp/pat

log "Cloning secrets to ~/.ssh..."
gh repo clone "$SECRETS_REPO" ~/.ssh 2>/dev/null || {
    # If ~/.ssh exists, pull instead
    git -C ~/.ssh pull
}
chmod 700 ~/.ssh
find ~/.ssh -name "id_*" ! -name "*.pub" -exec chmod 600 {} \;
find ~/.ssh -name "*.pub" -exec chmod 644 {} \;

log "Done. SSH keys in ~/.ssh, gh authenticated."

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bootstrap.sh — One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs
# Usage: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
#
# Phases:
#   1. Foundation — install base packages + yay (no auth, no input)
#   2. Secret Unlock — decrypt vault (one password prompt via /dev/tty)
#   3. Identity — deploy SSH keys, git config, gh auth (automatic)
#
# Environment variables:
#   VAULT_PASSWORD  — skip interactive prompt (for CI/headless)
#   DRY_RUN=true    — print what would happen without executing
#   BOOTSTRAP_REPO  — override repo URL (default: SkogAI/bootstrap)

set -euo pipefail

# --- Constants ---
BOOTSTRAP_REPO="${BOOTSTRAP_REPO:-https://github.com/SkogAI/bootstrap.git}"
WORK_DIR=""
SECRETS_DIR=""

# --- Colors ---
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m'

# --- Helpers ---
log()  { printf "${GREEN}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
warn() { printf "${YELLOW}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }
err()  { printf "${RED}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*" >&2; }
info() { printf "${BLUE}[bootstrap]${NC} %s\n" "$*"; }

dry_run() { [[ "${DRY_RUN:-}" == "true" ]]; }

run() {
    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] $*"
    else
        "$@"
    fi
}

# --- Cleanup trap ---
cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up..."
    if [[ -n "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" && -d "${SECRETS_DIR:-}" ]]; then
        # shred plaintext secrets
        find "$SECRETS_DIR" -type f -exec shred -u {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
        rm -rf "$SECRETS_DIR"
    fi
    if [[ -n "${WORK_DIR:-}" && -d "${WORK_DIR:-}" ]]; then
        rm -rf "$WORK_DIR"
    fi
    log "Cleanup complete."
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# --- Precondition checks ---
preflight() {
    log "Running preflight checks..."

    # Must be Arch Linux
    if [[ ! -f /etc/arch-release ]]; then
        err "This script is designed for Arch Linux only."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Must not be root
    if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
        err "Do not run as root. The script uses sudo where needed."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Network check
    if ! ping -c1 -W3 archlinux.org &>/dev/null; then
        err "No network connectivity. Check your connection."
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Preflight checks passed."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 1: Foundation
# =============================================================================
phase_foundation() {
    log "=== Phase 1: Foundation ==="

    # Full system upgrade
    log "Upgrading system..."
    run sudo pacman -Syu --noconfirm

    # Install essential packages
    local packages=(
        git
        base-devel
        openssh
        github-cli
        ansible-core
    )
    log "Installing packages: ${packages[*]}"
    run sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm "${packages[@]}"

    # Install yay if not present
    if command -v yay &>/dev/null; then
        log "yay already installed, skipping."
    else
        log "Building yay from AUR source..."
        if dry_run; then
            info "[DRY_RUN] Would build and install yay"
        else
            local yay_dir
            yay_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
            git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git "$yay_dir/yay"
            (cd "$yay_dir/yay" && makepkg -si --noconfirm)
            rm -rf "$yay_dir"
        fi
    fi

    log "Phase 1 complete."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 2: Secret Unlock
# =============================================================================
phase_secrets() {
    log "=== Phase 2: Secret Unlock ==="

    # Clone bootstrap repo (public, HTTPS, no auth needed)
    WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap.XXXXXX)"
    log "Cloning bootstrap repo to $WORK_DIR..."
    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would clone $BOOTSTRAP_REPO to $WORK_DIR"
        # Create fake structure for dry run
        mkdir -p "$WORK_DIR"
        return
    else
        git clone --depth 1 "$BOOTSTRAP_REPO" "$WORK_DIR/repo"
    fi

    local vault_file="$WORK_DIR/repo/secrets.vault"
    if [[ ! -f "$vault_file" ]]; then
        err "secrets.vault not found in repo!"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Decrypt vault
    SECRETS_DIR="$(mktemp -d /tmp/bootstrap-secrets.XXXXXX)"
    local decrypted_archive="$SECRETS_DIR/secrets.tar.gz"

    if [[ -n "${VAULT_PASSWORD:-}" ]]; then
        log "Using VAULT_PASSWORD environment variable..."
        echo "$VAULT_PASSWORD" | ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
            --vault-password-file /dev/stdin
    else
        log "Prompting for vault password (reads from /dev/tty)..."
        ansible-vault decrypt "$vault_file" \
            --output "$decrypted_archive" \
            --vault-password-file <(read -rsp "Vault password: " pw < /dev/tty && echo && echo "$pw")
    fi

    # Extract
    log "Extracting secrets..."
    tar xzf "$decrypted_archive" -C "$SECRETS_DIR"
    rm -f "$decrypted_archive"

    # Validate expected files
    local has_key=false
    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
        if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
            has_key=true
            break
        fi
    done

    if [[ "$has_key" == "false" ]]; then
        err "No SSH private key found in vault! Expected ssh/id_<type>"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat" ]]; then
        err "github_pat not found in vault!"
        exit 1
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" ]]; then
        err "gitconfig not found in vault!"
        exit 1
    fi

    log "Phase 2 complete. Secrets decrypted."
}

# =============================================================================
# Phase 3: Identity
# =============================================================================
phase_identity() {
    log "=== Phase 3: Identity ==="

    if dry_run; then
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would deploy SSH keys to ~/.ssh/"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would configure git identity"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would authenticate gh with PAT"
        info "[DRY_RUN] Would verify SSH and gh access"
        log "Phase 3 complete (dry run)."
        return
    fi

    # --- SSH keys ---
    log "Deploying SSH keys..."
    mkdir -p ~/.ssh
    chmod 700 ~/.ssh

    # Find and deploy SSH key files dynamically
    for keyfile in "$SECRETS_DIR"/ssh/id_*; do
        [[ -e "$keyfile" ]] || continue
        local basename
        basename="$(basename "$keyfile")"
        log "  Deploying $basename"
        cp "$keyfile" "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        if [[ "$basename" == *.pub ]]; then
            chmod 644 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        else
            chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/$basename"
        fi
    done

    # Deploy SSH config if present
    if [[ -f "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" ]]; then
        log "  Deploying ssh config"
        cp "$SECRETS_DIR/ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config"
        chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/config"
    fi

    # Start ssh-agent and add key
    log "Starting ssh-agent..."
    eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
    for keytype in ed25519 ecdsa rsa; do
        if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}" ]]; then
            ssh-add "$HOME/.ssh/id_${keytype}"
            break
        fi
    done

    # --- Git config ---
    log "Configuring git identity..."
    local git_name git_email
    git_name="$(grep '^name=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"
    git_email="$(grep '^email=' "$SECRETS_DIR/gitconfig" | cut -d= -f2-)"

    if [[ -n "$git_name" ]]; then
        git config --global user.name "$git_name"
        log "  user.name = $git_name"
    fi
    if [[ -n "$git_email" ]]; then
        git config --global user.email "$git_email"
        log "  user.email = $git_email"
    fi

    # --- GitHub CLI auth ---
    log "Authenticating GitHub CLI..."
    gh auth login --with-token < "$SECRETS_DIR/github_pat"
    gh config set git_protocol ssh

    # --- Verification ---
    log "Verifying access..."

    log "  SSH:"
    ssh -T git@github.com 2>&1 || true  # github returns exit 1 even on success

    log "  GitHub CLI:"
    gh auth status

    log "Phase 3 complete."
}

# =============================================================================
# Main
# =============================================================================
main() {
    echo ""
    log "SkogAI Bootstrap"
    log "================"
    echo ""

    if dry_run; then
        warn "DRY_RUN mode enabled — no changes will be made."
        echo ""
    fi

    preflight
    phase_foundation
    phase_secrets
    phase_identity

    echo ""
    log "Bootstrap complete!"
    log "Next steps:"
    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/claude ~/claude"
    log "  gh repo clone SkogAI/skogansible ~/.ansible"
    echo ""
}

main "$@"

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs. Installs github-cli, decrypts a PAT from an ansible-vault, authenticates gh, and clones `skogix/secrets` to `~/.ssh/`.

## Usage

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
```

## How it works

1. `pacman -S` github-cli, uv, ansible-core
2. `curl` + `ansible-vault decrypt` the encrypted PAT (`pat.vault`)
3. `gh auth login --with-token` with the PAT
4. `gh repo clone skogix/secrets ~/.ssh`

## Vault management

```bash
ansible-vault encrypt pat --output pat.vault   # encrypt plaintext PAT
ansible-vault decrypt pat.vault --output pat    # decrypt to plaintext
```

## Environment Variables

- `VAULT_PASSWORD` — skip interactive vault password prompt (for CI/headless)

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs. Installs github-cli, decrypts a PAT from an ansible-vault, authenticates gh, and clones `skogix/secrets` to `~/.ssh/`.

## Usage

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
```

## How it works

1. `pacman -S` github-cli, uv, ansible-core
2. `curl` + `ansible-vault decrypt` the encrypted PAT (`pat.vault`)
3. `gh auth login --with-token` with the PAT
4. `gh repo clone skogix/secrets ~/.ssh`

## Vault management

```bash
ansible-vault encrypt pat --output pat.vault   # encrypt plaintext PAT
ansible-vault decrypt pat.vault --output pat    # decrypt to plaintext
```

## Environment Variables

- `VAULT_PASSWORD` — skip interactive vault password prompt (for CI/headless)

lines: -One-liner bootstrap system for fresh Arch Linux installations. Automates setup from bare Arch to fully configured with SSH identity, Git config, and GitHub CLI auth via an Ansible Vault-encrypted secrets bundle.
+One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux installs. Installs github-cli, decrypts a PAT from an ansible-vault, authenticates gh, and clones `skogix/secrets` to `~/.ssh/`.
-make encrypt     # Pack secrets/ → tar.gz → ansible-vault → secrets.vault
-make edit        # Decrypt, open shell in secrets/, re-encrypt on exit
-make verify      # Test that vault can be decrypted
-make clean       # Shred all plaintext secret files

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

One-liner bootstrap system for fresh Arch Linux installations. Automates setup from bare Arch to fully configured with SSH identity, Git config, and GitHub CLI auth via an Ansible Vault-encrypted secrets bundle.

## Key Commands

```bash
make lint        # Run shellcheck on bootstrap.sh
make encrypt     # Pack secrets/ → tar.gz → ansible-vault → secrets.vault
make decrypt     # Decrypt secrets.vault → tar.gz → secrets/
make edit        # Decrypt, open shell in secrets/, re-encrypt on exit
make verify      # Test that vault can be decrypted
make clean       # Shred all plaintext secret files
```

## Architecture

**bootstrap.sh** is the single entry point, run via curl pipe. Three sequential phases:

1. **Foundation** — `pacman -Syu`, install core packages (git, openssh, github-cli, ansible-core), build yay AUR helper
2. **Secret Unlock** — Clone this repo via HTTPS, decrypt `secrets.vault` with ansible-vault, extract SSH keys + GitHub PAT + gitconfig
3. **Identity** — Deploy SSH keys (`~/.ssh/`), configure git user, authenticate `gh` CLI, verify connectivity

**Key design properties**: idempotent (safe to re-run), `set -euo pipefail` strict mode, cleanup trap that shreds temp files on exit/interrupt, Arch-only (`/etc/arch-release` check), refuses to run as root.

## Environment Variables

- `VAULT_PASSWORD` — Skip interactive vault password prompt (for CI/headless)
- `DRY_RUN=true` — Preview commands without executing
- `BOOTSTRAP_REPO` — Override the default repo URL

## Secrets Layout (inside vault)

```
secrets/
├── ssh/id_ed25519      # SSH private key
├── ssh/id_ed25519.pub  # SSH public key
├── gitconfig           # Git name/email
└── github_pat          # GitHub PAT
```

Plaintext secrets are gitignored. `shred` is used for cleanup but is ineffective on CoW filesystems (Btrfs).

```

Down from 300+ lines and 6 files to ~40 lines and 3 files.

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# bootstrap.sh — One-liner bootstrap for fresh Arch Linux
# curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/bootstrap.sh | bash
set -euo pipefail

VAULT_URL="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SkogAI/bootstrap/master/pat.vault"
SECRETS_REPO="skogix/secrets"

err() { printf '\033[0;31m[!]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
log() { printf '\033[0;32m[+]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }

cleanup() { rm -f /tmp/pat.vault /tmp/pat; }
trap cleanup EXIT

# Preflight
[[ -f /etc/arch-release ]] || { err "Arch Linux only"; exit 1; }
[[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]         || { err "Don't run as root"; exit 1; }

# Install deps
log "Installing packages..."
sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm github-cli uv ansible-core > /dev/null

# Decrypt PAT
log "Fetching vault..."
curl -fsSL "$VAULT_URL" -o /tmp/pat.vault

if [[ -n "${VAULT_PASSWORD:-}" ]]; then
    echo "$VAULT_PASSWORD" | ansible-vault decrypt /tmp/pat.vault \
        --output /tmp/pat --vault-password-file /dev/stdin
else
    ansible-vault decrypt /tmp/pat.vault --output /tmp/pat \
        --vault-password-file <(read -rsp "Vault password: " pw < /dev/tty && echo && echo "$pw")
fi

# Auth and clone secrets
log "Authenticating gh..."
gh auth login --with-token < /tmp/pat

log "Cloning secrets to ~/.ssh..."
gh repo clone "$SECRETS_REPO" ~/.ssh 2>/dev/null || {
    # If ~/.ssh exists, pull instead
    git -C ~/.ssh pull
}
chmod 700 ~/.ssh
find ~/.ssh -name "id_*" ! -name "*.pub" -exec chmod 600 {} \;
find ~/.ssh -name "*.pub" -exec chmod 644 {} \;

log "Done. SSH keys in ~/.ssh, gh authenticated."

content.text:
Yeah, chezmoi makes sense as the next step. Once `~/.ssh` is in place from bootstrap, you can `gh repo clone` your dotfiles and `chezmoi apply` to get your actual working environment (i3, kitty, nvim, shell config, etc.).

The question is — should chezmoi be part of `bootstrap.sh` or a separate step? Looking at your history, you were doing `yay chezmoi` + `chezmoi init` + `chezmoi apply` manually every time.

Adding it to the end of bootstrap.sh would be ~3 more lines:

```bash
yay -S --needed --noconfirm chezmoi
chezmoi init skogix/dotfiles
chezmoi apply
```

But that means bootstrap needs yay (which means `base-devel` + building yay from AUR first), and right now we stripped that out. Alternatively, chezmoi is available via `pacman -S chezmoi` directly — no AUR needed.

Do you have a chezmoi dotfiles repo set up, and what's the repo name? I saw `skogai/dotfiles` in your history.

message.content: chezmoi is via pacman but also infested with "ai-bloat" and need to be looked over heavily. would chezmoi init skogai/dotfiles && chezmoi apply be enough to bootstrap it?

content.text:
Basically yes — `chezmoi init skogix/dotfiles --apply` does both in one command. But "enough" depends on what's in your dotfiles repo.

The risk is that your chezmoi config has templates, scripts, or `.chezmoiexternal` entries that pull in stuff you don't want or that fail on a fresh system (missing deps, wrong paths). Your history showed multiple `chezmoi apply` attempts (lines 41, 47, 48, 65, 72) which suggests something was broken.

Before we wire it into bootstrap, want me to look at your dotfiles repo to see what chezmoi is actually doing? I can check for run scripts, templates, externals, and anything that might need cleaning up.

content.text:
Right. So `chezmoi init` will prompt for name, email, github_user, personal/work/headless. That's a problem for a non-interactive bootstrap via `curl | bash`.

You could skip the prompts by passing the data directly:

```bash
chezmoi init skogix/dotfiles --apply \
  --promptString name="Skogix" \
  --promptString email="your@email" \
  --promptString github_user="skogix" \
  --promptBool personal=true \
  --promptBool work=false \
  --promptBool headless=false
```

Or simpler — just hardcode a `~/.config/chezmoi/chezmoi.toml` before running init. But that's getting into "cleaning up the dotfiles" territory which you said is out of scope.

For now, adding these two lines to the end of `bootstrap.sh` is probably fine — the prompts will read from `/dev/tty` just like the vault password does:

```bash
sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm chezmoi
chezmoi init skogix/dotfiles --apply
```

content.text:
Nice. `skogix.sh` is way tighter — the key insight is `ansible-vault view` pipes directly to `gh auth login --with-token`, no temp files, no cleanup trap needed.

One thing I notice: it creates a venv and installs ansible-core via `uv tool install`, but you're also installing `ansible-core` via pacman on line 3. You only need one or the other. If `uv tool install ansible-core` works, you can drop it from the pacman line. If pacman's ansible-core works, you can drop the uv venv stuff entirely.

Also `skogai` vs `skogix` — the repos in `bootstrap.sh` and `skogix.sh` use `skogai` but your actual GitHub is `skogix`. Which is correct?

content.text:
On point 2 — `gh repo clone` into `~/.ssh` is fine for the first run, but on re-runs it fails (dir exists). The current fallback `git -C ~/.ssh pull` is fragile too (dirty working tree, merge conflicts with local changes).

A few options:

- **Ansible `git` module** — handles clone-or-pull idempotently, can force reset, set permissions. Since you're already pulling ansible in, this is natural.
- **Plain git** — `git clone` if missing, `git -C pull` if exists. Simple but not truly idempotent (fails on dirty state).
- **rsync from a temp clone** — clone to tmp, rsync to `~/.ssh/`, always overwrites. Brute but reliable.

Ansible's `git` module + a `file` module for permissions is probably the cleanest since you want ansible in the mix anyway. A tiny playbook that does what 10 lines of bash would do, but correctly handles every re-run.

Want me to add a small ansible playbook for the secrets clone + permissions step?

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+
+sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm github-cli uv ansible-core chezmoi git
+uv venv --seed --clear
+source .venv/bin/activate
+uv tool install ansible-core
+
+ansible-vault view ./pat.vault | gh auth login --with-token
+ansible-galaxy collection install --requirements-file=.requirements.yml --force
+ansible-playbook playbooks/bootstrap.yml --ask-become-pass

content.text:
Got it. So the flow is now:

1. **`skogix.sh`** — the real entry point: pacman basics, vault decrypt, gh auth, install ansible collections, run playbook
2. **`playbooks/bootstrap.yml`** — ansible takes over: installs all packages from `vars/packages.yml`
3. **`bootstrap.sh`** — the curl-able version (still has the old secrets clone + chezmoi stuff)

The main gap: `bootstrap.sh` and `skogix.sh` are diverging. `skogix.sh` is the one that actually works with the new ansible setup, but `bootstrap.sh` is what gets curl-piped on a fresh system.

Seems like `bootstrap.sh` should just become `skogix.sh` — or rather, `bootstrap.sh` should do the minimal pre-ansible bits (pacman, vault decrypt, gh auth) and then hand off to ansible. The secrets clone to `~/.ssh` should probably move into a role too.

Is that where you're heading — merge these two scripts and make the secrets/ssh setup an ansible role?

message.content: pretty much yes, 1. git clone skogai/bootstrap 2. cd bootstrap 3. ./bootstrap.sh --- this should set up ansible and gh as is then ansible takes over so we can essentially install all the base packages, clone all the repos etc.

content.text:
Makes sense. So `bootstrap.sh` is just the pre-ansible glue — get gh authed, get ansible running — then ansible does everything else.

Right now the pieces are mostly there, just split across two files. Let me merge `skogix.sh` into `bootstrap.sh` and add a secrets role.

#!/usr/bin/env bash

sudo pacman -S --needed --noconfirm github-cli uv ansible-core chezmoi git
uv venv --seed --clear
source .venv/bin/activate
uv tool install ansible-core

ansible-vault view ./pat.vault | gh auth login --with-token
ansible-galaxy collection install --requirements-file=.requirements.yml --force
ansible-playbook playbooks/bootstrap.yml --ask-become-pass
